feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Apps
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Apps Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Please help me I have been stuck for months trying to get a toast to pop up using location
entry.content:
Basically I have it set up now where it will display the users coordinates when you push a button. Id like to have it so that when the users coordinates are being updated all the time (I downloaded the location polling GPS function from the marketplace) but I do not know how to make a pop up or a toast appear when the user is at a certain location. Id like to start with something simple like when they are about 20 meters away from a good restaurant in town. 

I have it so that when i push a button it shows my coordinates, but I dont understand how to compare those to the coordinates of a place in town. I have been reading the learning journys and watching videos. Iv been trying everything for so long now im getting really discouraged. 

I have Variables set up to get the users lat and longitude data, I also have set app variables to store the Hotel coordinates but i dont understand what im doing wrong. 

Screen shots below

I am a software developer, learning still clearly lol. Im surprised that I find I'm getting closer using Kotlin and android studio than I am using this supposedly easier method.

entry.title: Share data between SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Use Case:

  1. SAP Process Automation 1: regularly read elements via SAP GUI
  2. SAP Build Apps: allow users to update elements.
  3. SAP Process Automation 2: regularly write updated elements via SAP GUI

What is the best practice approach / reference architecture for persisting the data, allowing to share between SAP Build Apps # and SAP Build Process Automation?

entry.title: Sales Order and SAP BTP Use Case with SAP Build Apps, SAP Integration Suite and Sales Order API
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm currently working on my first Business Use Case using the SAP BTP abilities. What I would like to do is the following Use case : Anticipate delivery delay and inform the customer

I wanted to go trough those steps : 

  • Detection: The SAP system detects a delay or application.
  • Transmission: The event is sent to SAP Integration Suite through an API connection.
  • Preparation: SAP Integration Suite prepares the notification message.
  • Sending: Notification is sent to the client via email, SMS, or portal.
  • Confirmation: Receipt by the client is recorded.

I directly found my self in a bad spot about the delay detection and how to automate this step. So, I figured I had to simplify the use case in order to learn step by step the SAP BTP abilities and go with the automate delay detection later on. 

I finally chose to follow thoses steps : 

 

=> Develop a Build Apps Application and connect it to an Sales Order API from our S/4.

=> On the application, insert the product ID and the date on which the product will be delivered. 

=> Connected to the API Sales Order, we get the data about the product ID :

  • SalesOrder (key) : ID
  • RequestedDeliveryDate : Suggered delivery date by the system
  • DeliveryDateTypeRule : <blank>: The goods should be delivered on the requested delivery date or later. A: The goods must be delivered exactly on the confirmed delivery date. B: The goods must be delivered by the confirmed delivery date (that is, on the date or earlier).

=> We compare with the date input from the application to see if there will be any delay.

=>If there is any, we informe automatically the client by email (with intégration suite ?)

It brings me to the SAP community, to get an idea of the success possibility of this use case based on your opinions. To know if my choice of tools is accurate or if I could use any others, if the order in which I use them is accurate and if there is any advice or tips to be aware of before starting this use case.

Thank you all.

Regards,

Tom

entry.title: Problem in SAP Process automation
entry.content:
I am trying to open my project but I am getting a blank page.

I tried both my company account and my trial account and they act the same.

It is already 3 days like that.

It worked in both places before that.

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: SAP Build Apps BTP Destination Not Working When Deployed - Works in Preview
entry.content:
I have created the destination shown below, then connected it to Build Apps via Universal REST API integration. 

It works well when I click on test, or in the preview. 

However, when I deploy it and open it, the destination returns 500 without any explanations. 

Has anyone come across a similar issue?

entry.title: Compare Date on SAP Build Apps based on the Sales Order API
entry.content:
Hi All ! 

I'm currently working on a SAP BTP Use case project : Anticipate delivery delay and inform the customer about it.

I'm using SAP Build Apps connected to the OData Sales Order API (API_SALES_ORDER) and Right now, the application looks like this :

I'm using the Sales Order API, which has a data entity 'A_SalesOrder' with 84 fields, including SalesOrder (ID) and RequestedDeliveryDate.

The dates that you see on the screenshot were displayed by using this formula : 

 Knowing that RequestedDeliveryDate is Date/time text and a field from the A_SalesOrder entities (iso 8601) :

What I want to do is add two input fields to my application: input_ID (for the sales order ID) and input_date (for the new delivery date). I also have a search button, and when I press it:

I want it to find the order with this ID (data name: SalesOrder) and compare its expected delivery date (data: RequestedDeliveryDate) with the input_date.

I'm having trouble with the date and specially to compare them between each other.
How should I process to compare a basic date input (25.03.2024 for ex) with the RequestedDeliveryDate ?
I looked upon those but it does not work : 

- https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-build-apps-convert-date-from-odata/qaq-p/12784111 

- https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-end-to-end-scenario-use-cases/blob/main/topic1/build-apps/sap_build_apps.md 

Thanks for your help.

Regards,

Tom

entry.title: Send Image from SAP Build apps to SAP DoX service via SAP BTP Destination
entry.content:
Hello experts,
my use case is I'm using Take Picture action in Build apps to take a Photo of Invoice and i want to Send the Photo to SAP DOX service via SAP BTP Destination as i'm  getting CORS issue with Normal REST APIs. 
My doubt is How to include the image as a Form-data in the post request??? 
or is there any better way to do This use case Please suggest i'm open for anything!!!

@AnuradhaDhingan 
@aman175105  @Chaitanya_Priya

entry.title: Unable to launch SAP Build App
entry.content:
Hi,
I am trying access SAP Build Apps using my SAP trial account.
I have subscribed to SAP Build App using the booster. When I am trying to access the SAP Build App from subscription I am facing below error.
Could you please help me in fixing this issue



Thanks &Regards,
Sreeja.

entry.title: Configuring or Disabling Authentication in SAP Build Apps Deployed on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working with SAP Build Apps, which I have deployed on SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform). Whenever I access the application in SAP C4C, a login window pops up requesting a username and password.


Is it possible to completely disable authentication for my SAP Build Apps application deployed on SAP BTP?
I would prefer to have unrestricted access to the app without the need for user credentials, if that's feasible.

If complete disabling is not possible, can authentication be managed through Mashup parameters or some configuration settings?
Ideally, I would like to preset the username and possibly bypass the login screen through backend configurations or scripts.

Are there any recommended practices for managing authentication in SAP Build Apps within the SAP BTP environment?
If disabling authentication isn't an option, I am open to other suggestions that can simplify the login process for end-users.

Any guidance, examples, or documentation references would be greatly appreciated. I am relatively new to working with SAP Build Apps, so detailed steps or explanations would be very helpful!

entry.title: no access to SAP Build Apps Lobby via subaccount
entry.content:
My colleague is the admin of the central BTP account in our org and provided me a subaccount I d´like to to use for SAP Build Apps.
Roles of BuildAppsAdmin and BuildAppsDeveloper are set for me. Also all other settings are fine, nevertheless I can´t access the SAP Build Lobby and get the following error message

"Access Denied
You need permission to access this page. Request access from your administrator."

thank you

entry.title: SAP BUILD APPS is showing subscription error even with booster
entry.content:
Hi All, even after trying several ways BTP trial account is not able to subscribe SAP Build Apps, Can anyone help here.

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Where is data stored maintained in SAP Build Apps (from Visual Cloud Functions or from the App)
entry.content:
Hello

Questions: 

1/ Where is data stored maintained in SAP Build Apps (from Visual Cloud Functions or from the App) ?

Where and how can we choose the DB on which the data should be stored ? How can we easily do the sizing ?

2/ How can that data be accessed (assuming that it is stored on a DB that can identified)? How can that data be extracted ? Is there a SQL/CDS based access mechanism available ? 

3/ Can we develop SAP Build Apps on a HANA database on which we also develop SAP CAP or SAP RAP ? In other words, can we setup a CDS based data model in 1 SAP HANA DB and then develop above it using CAP or RAP or SAP Build Apps/SAP Build Code where the data model is shared ? (I know that this goes against microservices paradigm, but a coherent CDS based data model on which we develop using the appropriate technology seems to me a good compromise ....)  

Context: 

We are considering to use for example SAP Build Apps to enhance C4C V1 in preparation to move in the future to C4C V2. In this context we were wondering where the data of SAP Build Apps is stored ? Also how would that data be enabled for other usages ? Indeed, we might want to extract that data to Data Warehouse, extract back into C4C V1 for other enablement. It is however not at all clear on how that data can be accessed and exploited. For the moment the SAP Build Apps (for sure the backend features VCF) seems to be a huge black box. Making a nice application with low code/no code is marvelous. But if that is generating hidden data that is not accessible, then we have created a new problem. 

PS: It might be that I am thinking wrongly in the cloud context or that I missed something. My apologies if this would be the case.

entry.title: Change Logs when using Fiori Applications
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Recently we have started to look at activating Standard Fiori applications for standard business transactions where users can Manage documents like Sales documents, Purchasing documents and Billing documents. We have noticed few things and want to understand how can this can be handled in a better way.

  • Origin of transaction missing when creating documents from Fiori. For example when we post a JV in finance from Fb01 we can see in the header of the accounting document the transaction is FB01 or FB70. But when we use Fiori to post this we see this empty. I can understand that BAPI is used for all updates but will be good to have either the App id or some kind of reference populated. this becomes a audit issue to track from where the document originates. The same goes for Change logs in Sales documents - Missing Tcode on the change logs. 
  • All the custom screen validation that was done in our PBO/PAI are not being called and again I assume its due to BAPI. Do we need to completely redesign all the validations or is there any other smarter approach? 
  • Attaching workflow into Standard Fiori apps - Sometimes we also see that business needs to have a approval flow for some master data records before being created. We can go to SAP build and do it or create custom Fiori app and enable workflow. Has anyone done it with using the standard Fiori application and attaching a workflow for approval and then once approved the master data getting created?  (this can be more a custom requirement) 
  • Another this is that the user is not having VA01 or VA02 access in the PFCG roles but when we activated Manage Sales order Version2 app and try to use the user got access to create the Sales order for the document type enabled via Fiori. We have only added the UI roles and not changes the existing role of the user. Again is this because of BAPI and how well can this be restricted using standard capabilities?

 

Appreciate your insights.

 

Br,

Vijay V

entry.title: Issue displaying data in item list in Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi,

I need a hand regarding an app I'm creating in Build Apps.

I'm building a pretty straight-forward app for our clients to approve their own invoices. It is composed of 4 screens (Authentication, Invoice list, Invoice Details and Account Balance). Basically, after authenticating, the Invoice List page appears, then the user selects an invoice and is taken to the Invoice Details page where she/he can either approve or reject the invoice. If she/he approves the invoice, the number and amount of the invoice appear on the Account Balance page. Each time an invoice is approved, the related data is added to the Balance page.

So I manage to display an approved invoice in the item list of the Balance page. However, when I approve another invoice, it is not added to the previously approved one on the Balance page: it replaces it.

Below you will find several screenshots showing how I configured both Invoice Details page and Balance page: could you please give a look to see what I am missing please? I also joined a short video.

Here is the app variable I defined, named “approvedInvoice” (list of 3 objects), where I want to store the data of the invoices that are being approved:

Here is the logic flow I added to the Approve button ("Set app variable", where I added the app variable I created in the properties and assign the value with a custom list):

When I click on the Custom list of the Assigned value from the above screen, here is what I configured (DATA ENTITY TEST being my data source, the one from which I get all the data of my invoices that are display in the Invoices page):

Here is how I configured the Open Page properties ("approvedInvoice" being my page parameter):

And here you can see how I configured my list item in the Balance page:

See the video here for better understanding (N.B.: as you will notice, I'm currently displaying the client name in place of the amount, it is just temporary and does not change the problem raised in this post):

This is what happens when I approve more than one invoice: approved invoices don't pile up, only the latest approved one is displayed...

Would you be so kind and give me just a hint to solve this please? I'm stuck on this for a week now and it is blocking the whole project...

Many thanks beforehand for your help and I'm looking forward to reading you.

BR,

LM.

entry.title: Looking for app developers who monitor apps they deployed
entry.content:
Hi,

We're working on a new dashboard for application monitoring for frontend, backend, and fullstack developers. 

I'm looking for developers who monitor the application they deployed for short interview to better understand what info you would expect to see and what actions you would expect to be available for you.

No need to prepare anything. Only meet for 30 min interview and impact on dashboard design.

Your feedback is highly appreciated.

Ravid (ravid.ellenbogen@sap.com)

entry.title: Looking for app developers who monitor apps they deployed
entry.content:
Hi,

We're working on a new dashboard for application monitoring for frontend, backend, and fullstack developers. 

I'm looking for developers who monitor the application they deployed for short interview to better understand what info you would expect to see and what actions you would expect to be available for you.

No need to prepare anything. Only meet for 30 min interview and impact on dashboard design.

Your feedback is highly appreciated.

Ravid (ravid.ellenbogen@sap.com)

entry.title: Unable to perform POST from SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing error while performing  a POST request form SAP build apps to SAP Hana DB. The service was created using a CAPM project in SAP BTP.

Service works correctly using a postman. GET, POST, PATCH is working.

 

Below is  error "msg":"400 > /n code: '400",In message: 'Property I"@odata.type\" does not exist in Asp•••••••••••••.Asp●●●●●●●●"In)","type":"log"]

 

However, not from build apps.

entry.title: in my recently developed apps on sap build apps,I am having to refresh page to view effect.help?
entry.content: in my recently developed apps on sap build apps,I am having to refresh page to view effect.help?Every time I am upadating a field  in navigated page then I will have to comeback to main page to view it.It is supposed to show data automaticatically but I am having to refresh the page here. Need help..Thanks in advance.
